correction_notice: This manifest was rewritten (v0 -> v1) after a regression was caught and recovered. The first deletion pass relied on a literal-string reference scan ('staging/<path>') that MISSED tool-mediated and fixture-root-constructed path dependencies. Deleting all 33 archived trees broke 37 unittest cases (33 FileNotFoundError). Recovery: all 33 trees were restored from the external archive (407 tests passed = true baseline), then deletion was re-done EMPIRICALLY — only trees whose removal kept the full 407-test suite green were deleted. Net: 25 trees verified-safe-deleted, 8 trees kept (test-required).

external_archive.contents: ALL 33 originally-archived trees (superset). 8 of these were ultimately KEPT in the working tree; the archive still backs them up redundantly. 25 were deleted and remain recoverable here.

verification_method: After every deletion batch, ran the full suite: python -B -m unittest discover -s Iris/build/description/v2/tests -p 'test_*.py'. A batch was accepted only if result stayed 'Ran 407 tests ... OK' (EXIT 0, 0 FileNotFoundError). NOTE: earlier 'EXIT 0' claims via '| tail' pipelines were invalid (captured tail's exit code, not python's); exit codes here were captured with redirection, not pipes.

deleted_verified_safe.criterion: leaf-name reference count 0 in tests/ (direct), AND full 407-test suite stayed green after removal
deleted_verified_safe.note_second_pass_backlog_132: Deleted (archived 50 MB version removed). A build-test regenerates a minimal (~0 MB) version on test run; that regenerated output is ephemeral, gitignored, and harmless.

kept_test_required.criterion: leaf-name referenced by tests/ (directly) OR removal broke the 407-test suite; restored from archive and retained

deleted_outright_no_archive.reason: superseded by referent_recovery variant; only self-reference

lessons: Literal 'staging/<path>' grep is insufficient for dependency detection; tests reach staging via tool modules and fixture-root path construction.
Destructive disposition must be gated by the actual test suite, run with correctly-captured exit codes (redirection, not '| tail'), BEFORE claiming safety.
An external archive plus a green full-suite run is the real safety net; both were ultimately used.
